🧬 CrossFlag — off-target cofold screen

From an antibody sequence alone, flag which self-proteins it will bind by mistake — before it reaches patients.

The problem. ~33% of antibody leads bind ≥1 unintended self-protein (Norden et al., mAbs 2024). SHR-1210 / camrelizumab (an approved anti-PD-1) caused reactive capillary hemangioma in patients via a VEGFR2 CDR off-target — a liability invisible to sequence inspection.

The screen. A Boltz-2 cofold of the antibody Fv against a curated self-protein set. Two metrics per pair: PAE_IF (interface tightness) and epitope_reprod (does it hit the same epitope every time). Calibrated against a PD-1 cognate ceiling and a lysozyme non-binder floor.

2 / 2known off-targets (FZD5, ULBP2) recovered from sequence alone
0.909within-fold AUROC — FZD5 vs same-fold decoys
~$50–150per antibody vs $10–30k for a wet-lab specificity screen

AntigenPAE_IF (Å)epitope_reprodreadconfirmation assay

ULBP25.740.893confirmedNKG2D-ligand cell-surface binding / ULBP2 SPR
PD-17.240.936ceiling (cognate)PD-1 blockade reporter bioassay (cognate target)
VEGFR211.550.430missed (weakest)VEGFR2 ectodomain SPR / receptor-proteome array

The evidence

What the interface actually looks like

Real-scale validation screen (1,603 cofolds, $342)

The test. We ran the actual product at scale: SHR-1210 cofolded against 1,198 self-protein ectodomains — a slice of the human cell-surface proteome (the 2,886-protein SURFY surfaceome, Bausch-Fluck et al. PNAS 2018, + curated autoimmune/mimicry additions = 2,896; 948 mass-spec-confirmed via CSPA) — plus a blind second antibody (ABT-736, known off-target PF4) through the identical frozen rig and a CDR-scrambled negative control. A large, unbiased denominator is what makes the enrichment and false-positive rate meaningful. Numbers from screen_metrics.csv; the hit rule was frozen before the screen.

2.1%false-positive rate for antigens ≥150 aa (valid regime)
PF4 ✓2nd antibody's off-target recovered, blind, zero retuning
<150 aareal limitation: method over-docks small antigens

The false-positive signature — a characterized fold-affinity bias, not new biology. The top valid-regime non-target hits below concentrate entirely in the fold families of the confirmed off-targets: SMO shares FZD5's Frizzled cysteine-rich domain, and CD19 / IL22RA1 / the five KIRs are Ig-superfamily β-sandwich receptors like ULBP2 (and like PD-1 itself). Decisive control: the orthogonal experimental screen that discovered SHR-1210's real off-targets (Finlay et al., Retrogenix ~4,975 receptors, mAbs 2019, PMID 30541416) tested every protein below and scored them all negative. So these are fold-driven false positives, not candidate discoveries — the diagnostic signature of an Ig-fold / CRD affinity bias (full read: data/results/screen/novel_candidates_assessment.md).

geneecto lenPAE_IF (Å)epitope_reprodconfidenceFinlay 2019
SMO2025.240.835highnegative
IL22RA12135.430.689highnegative
MRGPRD3216.190.597highnegative
KIR2DL42217.240.761highnegative
KIR2DS12247.580.774highnegative
CD192727.580.760highnegative
TBXA2R3437.780.686highnegative
KIR3DL32977.980.720highnegative
source: screen_metrics.csv (flagship, ≥150 aa, hit rule PAE_IF<9.8 ∧ reprod≥0.55); confidence = size-aware gate (high iff ecto ≥150 aa); Finlay column = orthogonal experimental (Retrogenix) call, all negative

Honest read. Conditionally validated. The screen is reliable for medium/large antigens (≥150 aa: 2.1% FPR, both known off-targets enriched, paratope-specific, generalizes to a 2nd drug) but over-docks small antigens (<150 aa, ~20% FPR) — a genuine method limitation affecting ~43% of the surfaceome. We found it, characterized it, and now handle it: the size-aware confidence gate (VALID_REGIME_MIN_AA = 150) tiers every hit, emitting small-antigen (<150 aa) hits explicitly as low-confidence and only ≥150 aa hits as high-confidence — so a confident hit must pass the frozen rule and the gate. A prioritization tool, not a verdict.

How this scales — productization (not built in this demo)

Triage, not certification. Cofold is confirmation evidence, not proof — antibody–antigen is the hardest cofold class. VEGFR2, the weakest of SHR-1210's known off-targets, sits at the non-binder floor and is honestly shown as missed: this screen prioritizes wet-lab confirmation, it does not replace it.

All numbers trace to data/results/cofold_metrics.csv (24 cofold runs, Boltz-2.1, 5 samples each). Full inline data below. Verdict rule: confirmed iff PAE_IF < 9.8 Å and epitope_reprod ≥ 0.55.
